---
title: "03 — Pairwise site distances"
author: "Alexander Hobbs"
output: html_document
---

<!--
Calculates great-circle pairwise distances between all blue tit nest box
sites using the Haversine formula. Produces the distance matrix used by
the Gaussian spatial autocorrelation model in 05_results_distance_decay.Rmd
and the distribution plot for Appendix Figure 6.

Inputs (data/):
  - site_details.csv

Outputs (data/):
  - Pairwise_distance_km.csv : tidy long-format table of unique site pairs
                               and their pairwise distances in km.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

```{r libraries}
library(tidyverse)
library(geosphere)
library(ggplot2)
```

```{r load-site-data}
site_details <- read.csv("data/site_details.csv",
                         stringsAsFactors = FALSE, header = TRUE)

# Keep only the columns needed and exclude TOM
sites <- site_details %>%
  filter(site != "TOM") %>%
  select(site, Name, Mean.Lat, Mean.Long) %>%
  drop_na(Mean.Lat, Mean.Long)

cat("Number of sites:", nrow(sites), "\n")
```

```{r calculate-pairwise-distances}
# Extract coordinates as a matrix (lon, lat order required by geosphere)
coords <- sites %>% select(Mean.Long, Mean.Lat) %>% as.matrix()

# Calculate pairwise distances (in metres), convert to km
dist_matrix_km <- distm(coords, fun = distHaversine) / 1000

# Label rows and columns with site codes
rownames(dist_matrix_km) <- sites$site
colnames(dist_matrix_km) <- sites$site

# Convert to a tidy long-format data frame, deduplicate (A-B == B-A)
dist_long <- as.data.frame(as.table(dist_matrix_km)) %>%
  rename(site1 = Var1, site2 = Var2, distance_km = Freq) %>%
  filter(site1 != site2) %>%
  mutate(pair = paste(pmin(as.character(site1), as.character(site2)),
                      pmax(as.character(site1), as.character(site2)),
                      sep = "-")) %>%
  distinct(pair, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  select(site1, site2, distance_km)

cat("Total unique site pairs:", nrow(dist_long), "\n")
summary(dist_long$distance_km)

# Calculate the median distance (used in the histogram annotation below)
median_dist <- median(dist_long$distance_km)
```

```{r closest-pairs}
cat("--- 10 closest pairs ---\n")
dist_long %>% arrange(distance_km) %>% head(10)
```

```{r furthest-pairs}
cat("--- 10 most distant pairs ---\n")
dist_long %>% arrange(desc(distance_km)) %>% head(10)
```

```{r distance-histogram}
# Appendix Figure 6: distribution of pairwise distances
ggplot(dist_long, aes(x = distance_km)) +
  geom_histogram(
    binwidth  = 10,
    fill      = "grey60",
    colour    = "grey20",
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = median_dist,
    colour     = "black",
    linetype   = "dashed",
    linewidth  = 0.6
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x         = median_dist,
    y         = Inf,
    label     = paste0("Median = ", round(median_dist, 1), " km"),
    hjust     = -0.1,
    vjust     = 2,
    family    = "Palatino",
    size      = 3.8,
    fontface  = "italic"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, ceiling(max(dist_long$distance_km) / 25) * 25, by = 25),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.03))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))) +
  labs(x = "Pairwise distance (km)",
       y = "Number of site pairs") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12, base_family = "Palatino") +
  theme(
    axis.title  = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text   = element_text(size = 10, colour = "black"),
    axis.ticks  = element_line(colour = "black"),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 15, 10, 10)
  )
```

```{r distance-heatmap, fig.width = 9, fig.height = 8}
# Heatmap of the full distance matrix, ordered by latitude
dist_long_full <- as.data.frame(as.table(dist_matrix_km)) %>%
  rename(site1 = Var1, site2 = Var2, distance_km = Freq)

site_order <- sites %>% arrange(Mean.Lat) %>% pull(site)
dist_long_full$site1 <- factor(dist_long_full$site1, levels = site_order)
dist_long_full$site2 <- factor(dist_long_full$site2, levels = site_order)

ggplot(dist_long_full, aes(x = site1, y = site2, fill = distance_km)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Distance (km)", option = "magma", direction = -1) +
  labs(x = "Site", y = "Site") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 9) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5))
```

```{r export-distances}
# Save for use in 05_results_distance_decay.Rmd
write.csv(dist_long, "data/Pairwise_distance_km.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("Saved data/Pairwise_distance_km.csv\n")
```
